summary: The rate at which youth abscond from non-secure detention spiked in Fiscal 2024 before falling back down through Fiscal 2026. ACS attributes the recent improvement to added staffing support at non-secure facilities, more consistent staffing patterns, and more regular training and coaching on youth supervision; its reports do not spell out what specifically drove the Fiscal 2024 spike.

sources.label: ACS, Fiscal 2026 Preliminary Mayor's Management Report

oneLiner: increasing overall, though improving recently with added staffing support
